name: Strahd Von Zarovich
description: Count Strahd Von Zarovich is the ancient vampire lord and undisputed ruler of Barovia. Once a powerful conquering warlord of the Von Zarovich dynasty, Strahd made a dark pact to gain immortality after becoming consumed by jealousy over his brother Sergei's betrothal to Tatyana. He murdered Sergei at their wedding and pursued Tatyana, who threw herself from the walls of Castle Ravenloft rather than submit to him. The Dark Powers of the Shadowfell sealed Barovia in mist as his eternal prison. Strahd is a brilliant tactician, a skilled wizard, and a predator who toys with those trapped in his domain. He views the party as potential entertainment — or threats to be crushed.

name: Ireena Kolyana
description: Ireena Kolyana is the adopted daughter of the late Burgomaster Kolyan Indirovich and the sister of Ismark. Unknown to her, she is the latest reincarnation of Tatyana, the woman Strahd loved and lost centuries ago. Strahd has bitten her twice, and she bears the marks on her neck. Ireena is brave, compassionate, and determined not to become Strahd's prisoner. She is skilled with a sword and refuses to be treated as a helpless victim. The party's efforts to protect her from Strahd are central to the campaign.

name: Ismark Kolyanovich
description: Ismark the Lesser, as the villagers call him, is the son of the late Burgomaster Kolyan Indirovich and Ireena's brother. He carries the weight of his father's death and the responsibility of protecting his sister from Strahd. Although he bears the unflattering nickname due to his perceived inability to live up to his ancestor Ismark the Great, he is a capable warrior and a man of honor. He desperately seeks allies to help escort Ireena to safety.

name: Madam Eva
description: Madam Eva is the ancient leader of the Vistani encampment at Tser Pool and one of the most powerful seers in all the realms. She uses her Tarokka deck to divine the locations of powerful artifacts that can aid in the fight against Strahd. Despite her connections to the Vistani — who traditionally serve Strahd — Madam Eva appears to work toward his downfall for reasons of her own. Her readings are cryptic but always prove true. She is far older than she appears and harbors deep secrets about her connection to Barovia's dark lord.

name: Rudolph Van Richten
description: Dr. Rudolph Van Richten is the legendary vampire hunter who has spent decades tracking and destroying undead across the multiverse. He operates in Barovia under the disguise of Rictavio, a flamboyant carnival ringmaster, to avoid detection by Strahd's spies. Van Richten is methodical, experienced, and utterly dedicated to destroying Strahd, though he carries deep guilt over the Vistani curse placed on him after a tragic incident involving his son, Erasmus. The party met him at the Wizard Tower in Session 20 as Rudolph d'Avernir.

name: Ezmerelda d'Avenir
description: Ezmerelda d'Avenir is a young Vistani monster hunter and former student of Rudolph Van Richten. She came to Barovia searching for her mentor after they parted ways due to a disagreement. Ezmerelda is resourceful, fearless, and carries an arsenal of monster-hunting equipment. She lost her right leg below the knee to a werewolf and wears a prosthetic. Despite her youth, she is one of the most capable hunters in Barovia and a valuable ally against Strahd.

name: Rahadin
description: Rahadin is Strahd's loyal chamberlain and right hand, a dusk elf who betrayed his own people to serve the Von Zarovich family. He orchestrated the genocide of the female dusk elves on Strahd's orders, earning the eternal hatred of the surviving dusk elf community. Rahadin is surrounded by a deathly aura — the screaming phantoms of his countless victims that follow him everywhere. He is an exceptionally skilled warrior, utterly devoted to Strahd, and considers himself the count's most faithful servant.

name: Baba Lysaga
description: Baba Lysaga is an ancient, powerful witch who dwells in the ruins of Berez in a creeping hut perched on an enormous tree stump. She is obsessed with Strahd, believing herself to be his true mother, and has extended her life through dark magic for centuries to watch over him. Lysaga commands swarms of insects and scarecrows, and her magic rivals that of the most powerful spellcasters. She destroyed the village of Berez long ago in retaliation for the villagers' actions against one of Tatyana's reincarnations.

name: Kasimir Velikov
description: Kasimir Velikov is the leader of the dusk elves who live alongside the Vistani near Vallaki. He bears terrible scars — his ears were cut off by Rahadin as punishment for protesting against Strahd. Kasimir is haunted by visions sent by his dead sister Patrina, an elf who was stoned to death by her own people when she agreed to become Strahd's bride. He seeks the Amber Temple to find the means to resurrect her, unaware that Patrina's spirit has been corrupted by dark powers.

name: Baron Vargas Vallakovich
description: Baron Vargas Vallakovich is the burgomaster of Vallaki and a tyrannical leader who believes that weekly festivals of forced merriment will keep Strahd's evil at bay. His motto is 'All will be well!' and he punishes anyone who displays unhappiness with imprisonment in the stocks or worse. Despite his delusions, the Baron genuinely believes his festivals protect Vallaki. He rules through fear and his brutish enforcer, Izek Strazni. His wife, Baroness Lydia, dutifully supports his madness.

name: Lady Fiona Wachter
description: Lady Fiona Wachter is a Vallakian noblewoman who secretly worships devils and plots to overthrow Baron Vallakovich. She leads a cult of devil worshippers that meets in her home and maintains contact with Strahd, whom she views as Barovia's rightful ruler. Fiona keeps the preserved corpse of her dead husband in her bedroom and has two sons loyal to her cause. She is politically cunning and will use any means — including the adventurers — to seize power in Vallaki.

name: Izek Strazni
description: Izek Strazni is Baron Vallakovich's brutal enforcer and captain of the guard in Vallaki. He has a monstrous, oversized arm wreathed in barbs that can conjure fire — the result of a mysterious transformation during childhood. Izek is a sadistic bully who terrorizes the townspeople and delights in violence. He is secretly obsessed with Ireena Kolyana, commissioning the toymaker Blinsky to craft dolls in her likeness, though he doesn't understand why he feels drawn to her. Izek is Ireena's biological brother, separated in childhood.

name: Gadof Blinsky
description: Gadof Blinsky is Vallaki's eccentric toymaker whose motto is 'Is no fun, is no Blinsky!' Despite the grim nature of Barovia, Blinsky remains relentlessly cheerful and creates toys that are invariably creepy and macabre — headless dolls, coffin-shaped music boxes, and worse. He is accompanied by his pet monkey, Piccolo, a gift from Rictavio. Blinsky has been secretly commissioned by Izek Strazni to create dolls that look like Ireena, though the toymaker doesn't understand the reason for the requests.

name: Father Donavich
description: Father Donavich is the broken priest of the church in the Village of Barovia. His faith has been shattered since his son Doru returned from an assault on Castle Ravenloft as a vampire spawn. Donavich has kept Doru locked in the church undercroft, praying desperately for divine intervention that never comes. The constant screaming of his son, begging for blood, has driven the priest to the edge of madness. He refuses to leave the church or abandon his son, despite the danger Doru poses.

name: Doru
description: Doru is the vampiric son of Father Donavich, transformed into a vampire spawn after foolishly joining a group of villagers who stormed Castle Ravenloft. Strahd slaughtered the attackers but turned Doru as a cruel joke, sending him home to torment his father. Doru is emaciated and desperate for blood, chained in the church undercroft. Moments of his former personality still surface through the bloodlust, making his situation all the more tragic.

name: Morgantha
description: Morgantha is a night hag who, along with her daughters Bella Sunbane and Offalia Wormwiggle, operates out of the Old Bonegrinder windmill. The three hags disguise themselves as old women and sell 'dream pastries' to the desperate people of Barovia — addictive treats made from the life force of kidnapped children. The pastries grant vivid, happy dreams that provide temporary escape from Barovia's misery, but the cost is horrific. The party encountered the hags at Old Bonegrinder in Sessions 17-18.

name: Vladimir Horngaard
description: Vladimir Horngaard is the former commander of the Order of the Silver Dragon, now a revenant consumed by hatred. He and his knights once fought against Strahd but were defeated. Rather than finding peace, Vladimir's burning hatred keeps him and his fellow knights trapped as revenants in the ruined mansion of Argynvostholt. Ironically, Vladimir now opposes anyone who would destroy Strahd — believing the vampire deserves to suffer eternally in his prison of Barovia rather than finding release through death. The party visited Argynvostholt in Sessions 22-23.

name: Sir Godfrey Gwilym
description: Sir Godfrey Gwilym is a revenant and former knight of the Order of the Silver Dragon at Argynvostholt. Unlike his commander Vladimir Horngaard, Godfrey retains his honor and desire to see Strahd destroyed. He is willing to help the adventurers if they can find the skull of Argynvost, the silver dragon whose spirit still protects the mansion. Godfrey's loyalty to the order's true purpose — protecting the innocent from Strahd — makes him a potential ally despite his undead state.

name: Davian Martikov
description: Davian Martikov is the patriarch of the Martikov family and the secret leader of the Keepers of the Feather, a network of wereravens who operate as spies against Strahd throughout Barovia. The Martikovs run the Wizard of Wines winery, which produces the only wine in Barovia — a vital commodity. Davian is gruff, practical, and deeply committed to undermining Strahd's control. He and his family can shapeshift into ravens and often gather intelligence for those who oppose the dark lord.

name: Pidlwick II
description: Pidlwick II is a small clockwork effigy built to resemble Pidlwick, a court jester who once entertained the lords of Castle Ravenloft. The original Pidlwick was beloved by Duchess Dorfniya but grew jealous when a second fool was brought to court. Pidlwick II murdered the original jester and now wanders the castle, performing its programmed routines. It appears harmless and even endearing, but is capable of pushing creatures off ledges and other 'accidental' violence. It may follow the party if they show it kindness.

name: Kiril Stoyanovich
description: Kiril Stoyanovich is the alpha werewolf who seized control of the Barovian werewolf pack after imprisoning the former leader, Emil Toranescu. Kiril is brutal, savage, and completely loyal to Strahd, who granted the werewolves hunting rights throughout Barovia. He leads raids on villages and kidnaps children to be turned into new pack members. The party fought through the Werewolf Den in Session 21.

name: Emil Toranescu
description: Emil Toranescu is the former leader of the Barovian werewolf pack, imprisoned in Castle Ravenloft's dungeon by Strahd after Emil questioned the vampire's authority. His imprisonment allowed the more savage Kiril Stoyanovich to take control of the pack. Emil's mate, Zuleika, desperately seeks his release and may ally with the party to rescue him. Emil is more reasonable than Kiril and could potentially be convinced to turn the pack against Strahd.

name: Zuleika Toranescu
description: Zuleika Toranescu is a werewolf and the devoted mate of the imprisoned Emil Toranescu. She tends the shrine to Mother Night within the werewolf den and secretly opposes Kiril's brutal leadership. Zuleika prays constantly for Emil's safe return and is willing to help anyone who promises to free her husband from Castle Ravenloft's dungeons. She represents the more civilized faction within the werewolf pack.

name: Baron Krezkov
description: Baron Dmitri Krezkov is the burgomaster of Krezk, a walled village at the base of the Abbey of Saint Markovia. He is a cautious, protective leader who keeps Krezk's gates closed to outsiders. Krezkov's greatest sorrow is the recent death of his young son Ilya, which has left both him and his wife Anna devastated. He grudgingly allows outsiders in only if they can prove themselves useful to the village's survival. The party visited his domain in Sessions 22-23 while recovering the Amulet of Ravenkind from the Abbey.

name: The Abbot
description: The Abbot is a deva — an angelic being — who descended to Barovia centuries ago to bring hope to its people. He restored the Abbey of Saint Markovia and healed the sick, but centuries of exposure to Barovia's corruption and the Dark Powers twisted his mind. He now creates grotesque mongrelfolk from body parts, trying to build a perfect bride for Strahd, believing this will end the curse. His 'bride,' Vasilka, is a flesh golem he considers his masterwork. The Abbot's fall from grace is one of Barovia's greatest tragedies.

name: Luvash
description: Luvash is one of the leaders of the Vistani encampment outside Vallaki. He is a large, boisterous man consumed with worry over his missing daughter, Arabelle, who was kidnapped from the camp. Luvash will reward anyone who brings his daughter back safely. While the Vistani traditionally serve Strahd, Luvash's primary loyalty is to his family and his people.

name: Arrigal
description: Arrigal is Luvash's brother and one of Strahd's most trusted spies among the Vistani. While appearing friendly and helpful to outsiders, Arrigal reports everything he learns directly to Strahd. He delivered the false letter from the Burgomaster that lured the adventurers to Barovia. Arrigal is charming, clever, and deadly with a blade — everything a good spy should be.

name: Mad Mary
description: Mad Mary is a grief-stricken woman in the Village of Barovia whose daughter Gertruda went missing. She can be heard wailing from her boarded-up townhouse, inconsolable and half-mad with worry. Mary's screams echo through the empty streets, adding to the village's oppressive atmosphere. Gertruda actually left to find Castle Ravenloft, naively believing she could persuade Strahd to be kind.

name: Gertruda
description: Gertruda is Mad Mary's naive young daughter who left the Village of Barovia to seek out Castle Ravenloft, believing she could appeal to Strahd's better nature. Sheltered her entire life by her overprotective mother, Gertruda is dangerously innocent about the true nature of Barovia's lord. She now resides in the castle as Strahd's 'guest,' unaware of the mortal danger she is in.

name: Kaelin Stormraven
description: Kaelin Stormraven was a dark servant encountered by the party early in their journey. A member of the Stormraven bloodline, she wielded dark powers in service to malevolent forces. The party defeated Kaelin during Sessions 3-4 on the 27th day of Tarsakh. She was encountered initially in a church setting, appearing first as a lost girl before revealing her true nature. Her defeat was one of the party's first major victories.

name: Seraphine Stormraven
description: Seraphine Stormraven was a powerful member of the Stormraven bloodline who ambushed the party at Tser Pool. The Battle of Tser Pool during Sessions 15-16 on the 11th day of Kythorn was one of the campaign's most intense conflicts. Seraphine wielded devastating magical abilities and nearly overwhelmed the party before being defeated. Her death further weakened the Stormraven family's grip on the region.

name: Elandra Stormraven
description: Elandra Stormraven was the third and most savage of the Stormraven siblings encountered by the party. She had allied with the werewolf pack and was encountered at the Werewolf Den on the 18th day of Kythorn during Session 21. Elandra combined dark druidic magic with lycanthropic ferocity. Her defeat by the party eliminated the last of the active Stormraven threats.

name: Mistress Allyson
description: Mistress Allyson is a senior member of the Order of the Yellow Rose, a monastic order that guards one of the Keys of Shorthagot and the planar gateway beneath their citadel in Valls. She was rescued by the party during Sessions 6-7 when they arrived in Valls on the 30th day of Tarsakh. Mistress Allyson possesses deep knowledge of the Shadowfell gateways and the ancient threat of Shorthagot. She serves as a key information source about the campaign's overarching cosmic conflict.

name: Rudolph d'Avernir
description: The party encountered Rudolph d'Avernir at the Wizard Tower during Session 20 on the 17th day of Kythorn. A knowledgeable scholar of the arcane and the planes, Rudolph provided the party with crucial information about the magical forces at work in Barovia and the broader conflict involving Shorthagot and the Shadowfell. His tower contains a library of rare texts and magical artifacts. (Note: Rudolph's true identity may be connected to the legendary Van Richten family of monster hunters.)

name: Lord Geoffrey Frostmantle
description: Lord Geoffrey Frostmantle is a nobleman of the Frostmantle dynasty that has ruled Damara since the great reunification in 1285 DR. The Frostmantles are considered fair and just rulers who restored order after centuries of conflict. Lord Geoffrey is a capable warrior and diplomat who oversees the defense of Damara's borders. He maintains the ancient alliance with the Order of the Yellow Rose and takes the threat of the Shadowfell gateways seriously.

name: Prince Ian Frostmantle
description: Prince Ian Frostmantle is the heir to the Frostmantle dynasty of Damara. A young paladin trained in the martial and divine traditions, Ian represents the next generation of Damaran leadership. He is deeply concerned about the supernatural threats emanating from Barovia and the Shadowfell gateways. Prince Ian has been instrumental in rallying support for the party's mission and maintains close ties with the Order of the Yellow Rose.

name: Lady Elysra Nivariel Williams
description: Lady Elysra Nivariel Williams is a succubus who infiltrated Damaran society by marrying Lord Edmund Williams, a nobleman of Valls. By 1496 DR, she orchestrated the catastrophic Fall of Valls, breaching the Shadowfell gateway beneath the Order of the Yellow Rose's citadel. Her machinations unleashed dark forces that threatened all of Damara. Lady Elysra is a master manipulator who uses charm, seduction, and political intrigue to achieve her goals. Her true demonic nature is revealed in her transformed state.

name: Shorthagot, Harbinger of Ruin
description: Shorthagot is an ancient avatar of Tharizdun, the Chained God — one of the most terrifying entities in all existence. The Netherese Empire accidentally contacted Shorthagot through their Great Machine at Thul Avenar, and the entity was eventually imprisoned in the Shadowfell after the Time of Troubles. Its binding was split into four Keys scattered across Damara. Shorthagot's influence seeps through the weakened barriers between planes, corrupting everything it touches. It is the ultimate threat behind the campaign's events.

name: Father Alric
description: Father Alric is a priest who has accompanied or aided the party during their journey. A man of deep faith, he provides spiritual guidance and healing magic to those in need. His dedication to combating the undead and fiendish forces of Barovia makes him a steadfast ally in the fight against Strahd and the greater darkness threatening the region.

name: Velinka d'Avenir
description: Velinka d'Avenir is a Vistani woman connected to the d'Avenir family of monster hunters. Resourceful and street-smart, she serves as a guide and source of information about the Vistani people and their complex relationship with Strahd. Velinka walks a careful line between the Vistani traditions of serving Barovia's lord and her own moral compass.

name: Bragus Orentide
description: Bragus Orentide is a seasoned warrior and guard captain in the service of the Frostmantle dynasty. A veteran of numerous conflicts, Bragus is known for his unwavering loyalty and tactical acumen. He serves as a protector and military advisor, often found at the front lines whenever Damara faces threats from the encroaching darkness of the Shadowfell.

name: Edic Tholomen
description: Edic Tholomen is a scholar and wizard who studies the ancient Netherese ruins and the magical forces that permeate the region around Damara. His research into the Netheril Empire's Great Machine and the Keys of Shorthagot has provided crucial intelligence about the nature of the cosmic threat facing the world. Edic is soft-spoken but brilliant, often lost in thought about arcane theories.

name: Garrick Ironbrow
description: Garrick Ironbrow is a stout dwarven blacksmith and warrior who forges weapons and armor for those who fight against the darkness. His craftsmanship is renowned throughout Damara, and his weapons carry enchantments that are particularly effective against undead and fiends. Garrick is gruff but kindhearted, always willing to help those who stand against Strahd's evil.

name: Gorak Broken Tusk
description: Gorak Broken Tusk is a half-orc mercenary with a fearsome reputation. Named for his signature broken tusk, Gorak has survived countless battles through a combination of brute strength and surprising cunning. He works for whoever pays well but has a personal code of honor that prevents him from harming innocents. His allegiances in the current conflict remain fluid.

name: Haldor Stonehand
description: Haldor Stonehand is a dwarven cleric and craftsman who combines divine magic with exceptional skill in stonework and metalcraft. He serves as a spiritual leader among the dwarven communities of Damara and has dedicated his life to creating holy artifacts capable of combating the undead plague spreading from Barovia. His stone hand, the result of a petrification curse he only partially reversed, has become his namesake.

name: Mira Fleetfoot
description: Mira Fleetfoot is a halfling scout and spy who specializes in navigating the dangerous borderlands between Damara and Barovia. Quick-witted and light on her feet, Mira has an uncanny ability to slip past danger unseen. She serves as a courier and intelligence gatherer for the forces opposing Strahd, carrying messages between allied groups scattered throughout the region.

name: Olaera Moonsilver
description: Olaera Moonsilver is an elven enchantress who has studied the mystical barriers between the Material Plane and the Shadowfell for centuries. Her expertise in planar magic makes her invaluable in understanding the Mists of Ravenloft and the weakening boundaries that allow Shorthagot's influence to spread. Olaera is graceful, ancient, and carries the weight of seeing civilizations rise and fall.

name: Rurik
description: Rurik is a stalwart soldier in service to the Frostmantle dynasty. A man of few words but decisive action, Rurik has proven himself in numerous engagements against the dark forces threatening Damara. He is respected by his fellow soldiers for his bravery and reliability, always the first to volunteer for dangerous missions.

name: Selene Brightwater
description: Selene Brightwater is a half-elven bard and diplomat who serves as a mediator between the various factions opposing Strahd. Her silver tongue and magical music have defused tensions, rallied allies, and lifted spirits in even the darkest hours. Selene travels between settlements, spreading news, hope, and sometimes dire warnings about the growing darkness.

name: Ser Brennor Cale
description: Ser Brennor Cale is a paladin and knight sworn to protect Damara from the supernatural threats emerging from the Shadowfell. A man of unwavering faith and courage, Brennor has dedicated his life to the eradication of evil. He wears gleaming plate armor bearing the crest of his order and wields a blessed longsword. Brennor is among the foremost champions standing against the advance of darkness.

name: Thalin Emberbrew
description: Thalin Emberbrew is a dwarven ranger and master brewer whose fiery concoctions are as potent in combat as they are at the tavern table. Thalin knows the mountains and forests of Damara like no other and serves as a guide through the treacherous wilderness. His 'Emberbrew' — a dwarf-strength spirit — is legendary for its ability to warm even the coldest spirits and, rumor has it, can burn through a troll's regeneration.

name: Vera Thorn
description: Vera Thorn is a mysterious herbalist and hedge witch who lives on the borderlands between Damara and the Mists. She possesses knowledge of rare plants and potions, some of which can protect against the effects of Barovia's curse. Vera's patron and true allegiances are unclear — she helps those who seek her out but always at a price. Her thorny temperament matches her namesake.

name: Henrik Van der Voort
description: Henrik Van der Voort is the coffin maker of Vallaki, a nervous, frightened man who has been coerced by Strahd's agents into hiding vampire spawn in the upstairs of his shop. Henrik lives in constant terror, knowing that the creatures above could descend and kill him at any moment, but too afraid of Strahd's retribution to seek help. His shop has become an unwitting den of undeath in the heart of Vallaki.

name: Milivoj
description: Milivoj is a young gravedigger at the Church of St. Andral in Vallaki. Desperate to feed his younger siblings, he was manipulated by Henrik Van der Voort into stealing the sacred bones of Saint Andral from the church — the relic that protected the building from vampire entry. Milivoj is guilt-ridden but terrified of the consequences of his actions.

name: Tharizdun, The Chained God
description: Tharizdun is the Chained God, an ancient deity of madness, entropy, and eternal darkness who was imprisoned by the combined efforts of all other gods at the dawn of time. His very existence threatens the fabric of reality. Shorthagot serves as his avatar and harbinger, working to free the Chained God from his cosmic imprisonment. The four Keys of Shorthagot scattered across Damara are connected to the bindings that keep this terrible entity sealed away. Even imprisoned, Tharizdun's influence corrupts and drives mortals to madness.
